search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae hypertension telehealth clinical guideline PDF Dubai
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Single-drug sources do not support the broad follow-up workflow.
Dose-bearing treatment content was not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for adults with suspected or established hypertension.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth assessment and follow-up; emergency hypertension requires escalation.
UAE_applicability: Direct for Dubai DHA-licensed telehealth with local prescribing and referral governance.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 remains the current verified registry copy and is due for revision in 2029.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA issue was identified on the telehealth portal.

evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth hypertension scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped hypertension terms, blood-pressure review, blood-pressure examination, preset, and home-log review.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies office, repeated home, and ambulatory blood-pressure assessment and hypertension staging.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped home readings, adherence, lifestyle, history, and documentation-only plan or follow-up fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes monitoring phases, clinician-judged laboratory review, lifestyle domains, and monthly review context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped assessed chest, breathlessness, visual, and focal-neurological negatives.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists severe blood pressure, confusion, visual changes, chest pain, breathlessness, neurological symptoms, severe headache, and specialist referral criteria.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped renal, electrolyte, lipid, urine ACR, and ECG review.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists follow-up electrolytes, renal function, lipids, urine ACR, glucose or HbA1c, liver tests, blood count, and ECG according to medication and result context.

unresolved_source_gaps: Broad examination prompts beyond blood pressure and generic clinician-entered plans remain unsupported.
Diagnosis, target setting, medicine choice, dose, and emergency classification remain clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
